original_abstract: Azomethine imines serve as versatile building blocks in 1,3-dipolar cycloadditions with alkenes and alkynes, offering a robust synthetic route to various heterocycles including pyrazolines, pyrazolidines, pyrazolopyridines, and indazoloisoquinolines. These transformations are characterized by their high degrees of regio-, stereo-, and enantioselectivity, facilitating the streamlined construction of complex architectures such as pyrazolo[1,5-a]isoquinolines and pyrazolopyrazolones.
humanized_abstract: Azomethine imines are useful reagents for 1,3-dipolar cycloadditions with alkenes and alkynes to afford a wide variety of heterocyclic products such as pyrazolines, pyrazolidines, pyrazolopyridines, and indazoloisoquinolines. The corresponding reactions are highly regioselective, stereoselective, and enantioselective providing a straightforward access to complex molecules such as pyrazolo[1,5-a]isoquinolines and pyrazolopyrazolones.

document.output: Azomethine imines are useful reagents for 1,3-dipolar cycloadditions with alkenes and alkynes to afford a wide variety of heterocyclic products such as pyrazolines, pyrazolidines, pyrazolopyridines, and indazoloisoquinolines. The corresponding reactions are highly regioselective, stereoselective, and enantioselective providing a straightforward access to complex molecules such as pyrazolo[1,5-a]isoquinolines and pyrazolopyrazolones.
